ref: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
text: Aatikiti nwɔ lekoto osie kanya aabuɛ alɛ, “Obi sankobi nii koloo okpi. Lekoto wa faabatika nwɔ kɔnɛɛ di suoto di ofiɛ kuyɔɔ nwɔ, aawɛ nkpa.”
translated: The tortoise was going to the farm one day when he said, "No one can outsmart the koloo nut. The tortoise will return to this very spot and crack it open right here, just wait."

ref: After finding a very valuable one, the owner goes and sells everything in order to buy that pearl. A Fish Net
text: Obe wɔ aawɛ ni onii wɔ ninlɛ kulenke ni yi, aasifi aalaayɔ asaa nɔɔ ya amuu ninkpe nwɔ ni aasunsũ, aayɔ aalaaya oniibi nwu nkpo. Yesu Lapɛ Lɛkpa Aakyeko Asawu Bataatɛ Banwii Suoto.
translated: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep. The hired hand is not the shepherd and does not own the sheep. So when he sees the wolf coming, he abandons the sheep and runs away. I am the good shepherd; I know my sheep and my sheep know me. John 10:11, 12, 14.

ref: He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, “Can't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour?
text: Ni aakple aawa basaateketetɛ nɔɔ batiɛ nwu nkpo nfũ nɛ, aabanya ma baatɔɔkyɔsĩi. Ninfɛ aakaalɛ Petro alɛ, “Ni bilaafuo osiɛ otoonyunkoe lebombo ninwii pɛ?
translated: If you had asked me, I would have given you living water. But Peter said to him, "How can you, being a Jew, ask a Samaritan woman for a drink?"

ref: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Nfanwu anu lɛma niatikiti. Ninfɛ di Yesu lakpee ma kufiofa osie kanya alɛ, “Bitambuɛ nle mmle biatɔkɔ otii kuonwii.”
translated: One day Jesus was teaching his disciples. He said to them, "The Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies."

ref: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
text: Yesu lakple aabuɛ alɛ, “Be suoto kayɔ Yaa sɛka kalekɔ nkaatɛɛsa? Lɛmɛnkpa kayɔ ndiki ni kasɔ?
translated: Then Jesus asked them, “Which of you, if your son asks for bread, will give him a stone? Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a snake?

ref: The blind are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People with leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
text: Banumbiɛtɛ nfɛ kɔnya asaa, abafa nfɛ kɔtaka mankyɛ, batii ba ninkofiɛ ni sifiɛ sɛɛle suoto ntɔɔkpa, batokotĩitɛ nfɛ konu, bakpi kɔtaka mansiɛ nkpa, bapiitɛ kafɔɔ nfɛ konu ɔlaa biene nwu nkpo.
translated: The leopard's skin is beautiful, but it does not cover the body; the elephant's tusk is large, but it does not clear the path; the yam is big, but it does not solve hunger; the river is wide, but it can be crossed with a canoe; a person's strength is great, but they cannot fight alone.

ref: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
text: Baafuo otofo ta Farisi batii ku Yuda batii latɔɔbla bawa lɛma amanle ya baadiɛ baatɛ ma nii. Nioso diediki beefoto nnɛɛ lɛma fɛ mmle baatuo ma nii, fiɛ manle alesaa.
translated: The Pharisees and the Jews among them did not want to leave their old customs to follow the new ways. But whoever follows the new ways will not be lost, so you must believe.

ref: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
text: Yesu lakple aayila aatɔɔkakatɛ ko ma fiɛ di Yuda wɔ ninle ni lefosi batii banyɔ nwu nkpo onwii lɛma lawa. Batii dikudi le di olebatatɛ banɔɔfo ku kufiofa batuotɛ ku okpoo banɔɔfo ladiki ni latikanko nwɔ baatɔɔwa. Baaklee nwosoku ku mpamii.
translated: Then Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. He said to them, "Sit here while I go over there and pray." He took Peter and the two sons of Zebedee along with him, and he began to be sorrowful and troubled. Then he said to them, "My soul is overwhelmed with sorrow to the point of death. Stay here and keep watch with me."

ref: Jesus heard them and answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
text: Yesu lanu ɔlaa wɔ baatoobuɛ nii, ni aadiki kanya alɛ, “Fablatɛ diefĩ batii ba ninoofiɛ nii, bafiɛtɛ di fablatɛ lefĩ.
translated: When Jesus got into the boat, his disciples followed him. Suddenly, a furious storm came up on the lake, so that the waves swept over the boat.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: “Nioso se otii owo lɛtɔkɔ fɔ alɛ, ‘Nyu! Nnya Kristo ninfũ!’ ee ‘Nyɔɔ ninfa!’, tanfũ nwɔ faanu.
translated: "So if anyone says to you, 'Look! Here is the Christ!' or 'There he is!', do not believe it."

ref: Jesus asked her what she wanted, and she said, “When you come into your kingdom, please let one of my sons sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
text: Ninfɛ di Yesu lakaalɛ nwɔ alɛ, “Be lɛsaa foomiɛ?” Ninfɛ di ɔsanko nwu nkpo latɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Komiɛ nlɛ, fabla fatɛɛ alɛ, nse fɛɛwa sɛka lɛfɔ ole yi, babi nii ba mmle banyɔ yi, onwii masiɛ di oletannɛɛ kyɛ lɛfɔ, onwii kafɔɔ masiɛ di kɔmɛntunnɛɛ kyɛ lɛfɔ.”
translated: When Jesus came down from the mountain, large crowds followed him. Then a man with leprosy came and knelt before him, saying, “Lord, if you are willing, you can make me clean.” Jesus reached out his hand and touched the man. “I am willing,” he said. “Be clean!” And immediately he was cleansed of his leprosy.

ref: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
text: Bita yii lee dibla yii, biɛta oowo lee dibla oowo. Nle mmle kamaa, lɛsaa lele biekple biebuɛ kyiditɛ nwu nfũ diekye. Yesu Latuo Asaa Alɛ Butambla Okpile Buakyisa.
translated: Everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and he who humbles himself will be exalted. For I tell you, unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven. Jesus Christ the Son of the Living God the Savior of the World.

ref: After finding a very valuable one, the owner goes and sells everything in order to buy that pearl. A Fish Net

Jesus Christ, the Faithful Witness, the Firstborn from the dead, and the Ruler of the kings of the earth.

ref: Jesus then warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
text: Ninfɛ di Yesu latɔkɔ ma alɛ, “Bilolaa binyu nwaa di Farisi batii ku Saduki batii kɔfa ko mankpee ni di nnaafũ kamɛ nintaka ni suoto.”
translated: Then Jesus said to them, “Take heed and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and the Sadducees.”

ref: Jesus sent out the twelve apostles with these instructions: Stay away from the Gentiles and don't go to any Samaritan town.
text: Yesu lakpee basuɔtɔ lefosi banyɔ ba mmle kɔtɔ ku kufiofa ko mmle alɛ, “Bitankyɛ batii ba ninanle ni Yuda batii kasɔ ku Samaria batii okpoo kuonwii kafɔɔ kamɛ.
translated: And Jesus said to them, "You will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth."

ref: And unless you are willing to take up your cross and follow me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: Otii lele ninan-yɔ atoo nɔɔ atuka, atikanko nnafukpaa nii yi, aasiɛko alɛ aale mi saateketetɛ.
translated: When the sun rises in the morning, the birds sing beautifully, and the cool breeze gently touches my skin.

ref: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
text: Ninfɛ di Yesu ladiki kanya alɛ. “Be oso fɔɔkaalɛ mi ɔlaa fankyeko lɛsaa biene ɔbla suoto? Yaa lete ninle otii biene. Nse foomiɛ falɛ fawɛ nkpa be ninnaa ni kaloo yi, ni faale afiofa nɔɔ nwu nkpo.”
translated: When Jesus was a boy, he asked, "Why were you searching for me? Didn't you know I had to be in my Father's house?" But they did not understand what he was saying to them.

ref: He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, “Can't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour?
text: Ni aakple aawa basaateketetɛ nɔɔ batiɛ nwu nkpo nfũ nɛ, aabanya ma baatɔɔkyɔsĩi. Ninfɛ aakaalɛ Petro alɛ, “Ni bilaafuo osiɛ otoonyunkoe lebombo ninwii pɛ?
translated: If you had listened to my words and kept them in your hearts, you would not have stumbled. But Peter said to him, "Why has your heart doubted these things?"

ref: The same thing happened to the second and third brothers and finally to all seven of them.
text: Lɛsaa nwu nkpo kafɔɔ labla obilɛma suɔtobi nyɔɔfa nwu nkpo ku otiɛfa, dialaase di okuɛnsĩ le suoto. Kuonwii lɛma ditalofo ku ɔsanko nwu nkpo fiɛ baakpi.
translated: The hunter who goes to the forest does not know that the animal he seeks is also hunting him, so he must be cautious in the bush. The wisdom of our ancestors is like a baobab tree that one person cannot embrace.

ref: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Batii dikudi nwu nkpo lasĩ ɔlaa baata banumbiɛtɛ nwu nkpo osie kanya alɛ bamaasa kanya. Kafɔɔ mia nwu nfɛ baatɛ baafaa osie kanya baabuɛ alɛ, “Saate loo! Ɔka Dawid Obi ee! Nya wo nyaami kyu woo!”
translated: The men and women of the city ran to the road to see him as he passed by. When some of the people who were standing there heard this, they said, “Listen! It is David Obi! Let us go and meet him!”
